episodes.passage_iast: ya enaṃ vetti hantāraṃ yaś cainaṃ manyate hatam
episodes.passage_devanagari: य एनं वेत्ति हन्तारं यश्चैनं मन्यते हतम्
episodes.passage_english_gloss: He who thinks this Self is a slayer and he who thinks it is slain
episodes.correct_answer: The Self neither slays nor is it slain — 'hantāram' refers to the agent of action, not the true subject
partial_credit_answers: The verse concerns action and non-action

commentary_data.hantāram: Śaṅkarācārya: 'hantā' here refers to the buddhi, the apparent agent, not the ātman
commentary_data.2.19: This verse establishes the doctrine of ātman as akartā — the non-doer

candidate_options: The Self neither slays nor is it slain — 'hantāram' refers to the agent of action, not the true subject
The verse describes physical combat and the ethics of killing
The verse concerns action and non-action
Both slaying and being slain are valid interpretations depending on context

episodes.passage_iast: agniḥ sarvaṃ pacati dhātuṣu
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Agni digests everything in the bodily tissues
episodes.correct_answer: 'agniḥ' refers to the digestive fire (jaṭharāgni) that transforms nutrients within the seven dhātus
partial_credit_answers: Agni here means the general element of fire

commentary_data.agniḥ: Dalhana: agni here is specifically jaṭharāgni

candidate_options: 'agniḥ' refers to the digestive fire (jaṭharāgni) that transforms nutrients within the seven dhātus
Agni here means the general element of fire
The verse describes ritual fire offerings
Agni refers to inflammation in the tissues

episodes.passage_iast: sa rāmaḥ pitaraṃ natvā vanaṃ jagāma
episodes.passage_devanagari: स रामः पितरं नत्वा वनं जगाम
episodes.passage_english_gloss: That Rama, having bowed to his father, went to the forest
episodes.correct_answer: The pronoun 'sa' refers to Rama who departed for exile after receiving his father's blessing
partial_credit_answers: 'sa' could refer to any male character present
candidate_options: The pronoun 'sa' refers to Rama who departed for exile after receiving his father's blessing
'sa' refers to Lakshmana who accompanied Rama
'sa' could refer to any male character present
The verse describes Dasharatha going to the forest

episodes.passage_iast: prakṛteḥ kriyamāṇāni guṇaiḥ karmāṇi sarvaśaḥ
episodes.passage_english_gloss: All actions are performed by the gunas of prakrti
episodes.correct_answer: The compound 'kriyamāṇāni' splits as 'kriya + māṇa + āni' — passive present participle meaning 'being done'
partial_credit_answers: It is a simple verb form meaning 'doing'

commentary_data.3.27: Actions are attributed to prakṛti's guṇas, not the Self

candidate_options: The compound 'kriyamāṇāni' splits as 'kriya + māṇa + āni' — passive present participle meaning 'being done'
The word is an active participle meaning 'the doer of actions'
It is a simple verb form meaning 'doing'
The compound means 'actions that will be done in future'

episodes.passage_iast: kośo hi rājñaḥ pradhānam aṅgam
episodes.passage_english_gloss: The treasury is indeed the chief limb of the king
episodes.correct_answer: 'kośa' here means the state treasury (rājakośa), the primary resource for governance
partial_credit_answers: 'kośa' could mean a repository or sheath

'kośa' refers to the five sheaths of the self (pañcakośa)
The verse describes military equipment storage

episodes.passage_iast: bhūmermadhyāt sahasrāṃśoḥ khamadhyam upagacchati
episodes.passage_english_gloss: From the center of the earth, the thousand-rayed one reaches the mid-sky
episodes.correct_answer: 'bhūmermadhyāt' splits as 'bhūmeḥ + madhyāt' via visarga sandhi — 'from the middle of the earth'
partial_credit_answers: The compound describes a location but the split is unclear

'bhūmer + madhyāt' is a tatpuruṣa meaning 'earth-center'
The compound describes a location but the split is unclear
episodes.passage_iast_noisy: bhūmermadhyāt sahasramśoḥ khamadhyam upagacchati

episodes.passage_iast: yadā yadā hi dharmasya glānir bhavati bhārata
episodes.passage_devanagari: यदा यदा हि धर्मस्य ग्लानिर्भवति भारत
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Whenever there is a decline of dharma, O Bharata
episodes.correct_answer: 'glāniḥ' signifies a systemic decline or decay of cosmic order, not merely individual moral failure
partial_credit_answers: 'glāniḥ' means weakness or fatigue

episodes.passage_iast: vātapittakaphaś caiva tridoṣā iti saṃjñitāḥ
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Vata, Pitta, and Kapha — these are known as the three doshas
episodes.correct_answer: 'vātapittakaphaś' splits as 'vāta + pitta + kaphaḥ + ca + eva' — a dvandva compound listing the three doṣas
partial_credit_answers: It is a simple list of three terms

episodes.passage_iast: hanūmān sāgaraṃ tīrtvā laṅkāṃ prāpya sa vānaraḥ
episodes.passage_devanagari: हनूमान् सागरं तीर्त्वा लङ्कां प्राप्य स वानरः
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Hanuman, having crossed the ocean and reached Lanka, that monkey...

episodes.passage_iast: kṣetrajñaṃ cāpi māṃ viddhi sarvakṣetreṣu bhārata
episodes.passage_devanagari: क्षेत्रज्ञं चापि मां विद्धि सर्वक्षेत्रेषु भारत
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Know Me also as the Knower of the Field in all fields, O Bharata
episodes.correct_answer: 'kṣetrajña' is the conscious principle (ātman/puruṣa) that knows the body-field — Krishna identifies himself with this universal knower

'kṣetrajña' means the owner of the land
The verse describes agricultural knowledge

episodes.passage_iast: madhyamaṃ ravicandrāṇāṃ sphuṭaṃ kurvan vidhānataḥ
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Making the mean positions of the sun and moon true, according to rule
episodes.correct_answer: 'ravicandrāṇāṃ' splits as 'ravi + candrāṇām' — genitive plural dvandva meaning 'of the sun and moons/planets'
partial_credit_answers: It means 'of the sun-moon' as a single compound

'ravicandrāṇāṃ' is an avyayībhāva compound

episodes.passage_iast: sa vaidyaḥ rogaṃ jñātvā cikitsāṃ kuryāt

episodes.passage_iast: karmaṇy evādhikāras te mā phaleṣu kadācana
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Your right is to action alone, never to its fruits

partial_credit_answers: The compound simply means 'right to work'

episodes.passage_iast: ko nv asmin sāmprataṃ loke guṇavān kaś ca vīryavān
episodes.passage_devanagari: को न्वस्मिन् साम्प्रतं लोके गुणवान् कश्च वीर्यवान्

episodes.passage_iast: ko nv asmin sāmprataṃ loke guṇavān kaś ca vīryavān
episodes.passage_devanagari: को न्वस्मिन् साम्प्रतं लोके गुणवान् कश्च वीर्यवान्
episodes.passage_english_gloss: Who in this world today is virtuous and who is valorous?
episodes.correct_answer: Vālmīki asks Nārada about the ideal man — the two 'kaḥ' pronouns seek a single person embodying both guṇa and vīrya
partial_credit_answers: The verse asks about two different people

commentary_data.1.2: This is the praśna that initiates the entire Rāmāyaṇa

candidate_options: Vālmīki asks Nārada about the ideal man — the two 'kaḥ' pronouns seek a single person embodying both guṇa and vīrya
The verse asks about two different people
The pronouns refer to gods, not humans
'kaḥ' is rhetorical and expects no answer

episodes.passage_english_gloss: The sixfold policy is the chief instrument of kings for victory
episodes.correct_answer: 'ṣāḍguṇya' refers to the six measures of foreign policy: sandhi, vigraha, yāna, āsana, dvaidha, saṃśraya
partial_credit_answers: 'ṣāḍguṇya' means six virtues

ṣāḍguṇya.meaning: sixfold policy (sandhi, vigraha, yāna, āsana, dvaidha, saṃśraya)

candidate_options: 'ṣāḍguṇya' refers to the six measures of foreign policy: sandhi, vigraha, yāna, āsana, dvaidha, saṃśraya
'ṣāḍguṇya' means six virtues
The word describes six types of military formations
'ṣāḍguṇya' refers to the six seasons relevant to warfare
